title: Using long-term access keys is security-sensitive
description: Long-term AWS access keys are credentials that do not expire and can be used to access AWS resources. Using such keys increases the risk of compromise, as they can be leaked, stolen, or inadvertently committed to source control. It is recommended to use temporary credentials, such as those provided by IAM roles or AWS STS, to minimize the attack surface and improve security.
recommendation: Avoid using long-term AWS access keys in your code or configuration. Use IAM roles or temporary credentials whenever possible. Regularly rotate and audit access keys, and remove unused keys immediately.
impact: If long-term access keys are compromised, attackers can gain persistent access to AWS resources, potentially leading to data breaches, resource manipulation, or financial loss.

examples.good: Use IAM roles or temporary credentials provided by AWS STS instead of hardcoding long-term access keys.

checks.message: Avoid using long-term AWS access keys in your code or configuration. Use IAM roles or temporary credentials whenever possible. Regularly rotate and audit access keys, and remove unused keys immediately.
